question: A resource group can be created inside another resource group.
explanation: Resource groups cannot be nested. Each resource belongs to a single resource group, and resource groups serve as the management and RBAC scope. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/overview

question: An Azure free account has a spending limit.
explanation: The free account enforces a spending limit to avoid charges while you consume the promotional credit. When the limit is reached, resources are stopped until you remove the limit or upgrade to pay-as-you-go. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/spending-limit

question: An Azure free account has a limit of 2 TB of data that can be uploaded to Azure.
explanation: The free account is governed by credit and time (e.g., 30 days and promotional credit), not by a fixed limit of 2 TB of uploaded data. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/free/

question: An Azure free account can contain an unlimited number of web apps.
explanation: App Service plans and the free offer have quotas and limits (instances, CPU, connections, etc.). It is not unlimited. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/overview

question: Azure Pay-As-You-Go pricing is an example of CapEx.
explanation: Pay-As-You-Go is operating expense (OpEx): you pay per use monthly with no upfront capital investment. CapEx refers to purchasing assets such as hardware. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/cloud-adoption/getting-started/what-is-cloud-adoption#capex-vs-opex

question: Paying electricity for our datacenter is an example of OpEx.
explanation: Electricity cost is a recurring operating expense (OpEx) associated with running the datacenter.

question: Deploying your own datacenter is an example of CapEx.
explanation: Deploying your own datacenter requires upfront investment in assets (hardware, facilities), which is classified as capital expenditure (CapEx).

question: Match each service with its description.
right: Provide a portable environment for containerized applications
Used to build, deploy, and scale web apps
Provide a platform for serverless code

mapping.Azure Container Instances: Provide a portable environment for containerized applications
mapping.Azure App Service: Used to build, deploy, and scale web apps
mapping.Azure Functions: Provide a platform for serverless code

explanation: The correct matches reflect each service role: VMs (IaaS) virtualize the OS; Azure Container Instances runs containers without an orchestrator; App Service hosts managed web apps; Functions runs serverless code on demand. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/guide/technology-choices/compute-options

question: Azure Firewall will encrypt all network traffic sent from Azure to the Internet.
explanation: Azure Firewall inspects/filters traffic at network/application level, but it does not encrypt traffic. Encryption is provided with TLS/IPsec between clients and servers. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/firewall/overview

question: A network security group (NSG) will encrypt all the network traffic sent from Azure to the Internet.
explanation: A network security group (NSG) applies allow/deny rules (layer-4 firewall) and does not encrypt traffic. Encryption is done with protocols such as TLS/SSL or IPsec. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview

question: Azure virtual machines that run Windows Server 2016 can encrypt the network traffic sent from the virtual machines to a host on the Internet.
explanation: Virtual machines can encrypt traffic using TLS/SSL (application) or IPsec (network) when communicating with Internet hosts. This depends on system/application configuration.

question: Select the correct Azure portal URL.
explanation: The Azure management portal is https://portal.azure.com. The other domains are not the management portal.

question: Azure Security Center can monitor Azure resources and on-premises resources.
explanation: Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly Security Center) can assess resources in Azure and hybrid/on-prem environments via Azure Arc. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/defender-for-cloud/

question: All Azure Security Center features are free.
explanation: Defender for Cloud provides basic recommendations for free, but advanced capabilities (protection, alerts, etc.) require paid plans. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/defender-for-cloud/defender-for-cloud-introduction

question: From Azure Security Center, you can download a Regulatory Compliance report.
explanation: The Regulatory compliance dashboard lets you view and export assessments against frameworks such as ISO and PCI. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/defender-for-cloud/defender-for-cloud-compliance

question: _____ provides a common platform for deploying objects to a cloud infrastructure and for implementing consistency across the Azure environment.
explanation: Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates define infrastructure declaratively (IaC) and enable repeatable, consistent deployments. They are the basis for automation with version control. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/templates/overview

question: You can associate a network security group (NSG) to a virtual network subnet.
explanation: NSGs can be associated with subnets and VM NICs. Associating an NSG with a subnet applies its rules to all interfaces of resources within that subnet. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview

question: You can associate a network security group (NSG) to a virtual network.
explanation: NSGs cannot be associated with the virtual network resource as a whole; only with specific subnets or individual NICs. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview

question: You can associate a network security group (NSG) to a network interface (NIC).
explanation: Besides subnets, an NSG can be applied directly to a VM NIC for more specific rules. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview

question: Data stored in the Archive access tier of an Azure Storage account _____
options: can be accessed at any time using azcopy.exe
can only be read by using Azure Backup
must be restored before the data can be accessed
must be rehydrated before the data can be accessed
right: must be rehydrated before the data can be accessed
explanation: The Archive access tier is offline/rare access. To read the data you must first rehydrate to Hot or Cool; this process can take hours and has a cost. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/blobs/access-tiers-overview

question: The composite SLA for an application with an Azure Web App (99.95%) and an Azure SQL database (99.99%) is _____
options: the product of both SLAs, which equals 99.94 percent
the lowest SLA associated to the application, which is 99.95 percent
the highest SLA associated to the application, which is 99.99 percent
the difference between the two SLAs, which is 0.04 percent
right: the product of both SLAs, which equals 99.94 percent
explanation: The composite SLA is calculated by multiplying independent availabilities: 0.9995 × 0.9999 ≈ 0.9994 (99.94%). This reflects that the application depends on both components. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/framework/resiliency/availability-sla

question: Azure Site Recovery provides _____ for virtual machines.
explanation: Azure Site Recovery replicates workloads and orchestrates failover between locations for business continuity in case of disasters. It does not provide real-time fault tolerance. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/site-recovery/site-recovery-overview

question: Azure Advisor can generate a list of Azure virtual machines that are protected by Azure Backup.
explanation: Azure Advisor provides cost, performance, reliability, and security recommendations, but not detailed backup protection inventories. Use Azure Backup Reports or a Recovery Services vault for that. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/advisor/advisor-overview

question: If you implement the security recommendations provided by Azure Advisor, your company's secure score will decrease.
explanation: Implementing security recommendations typically improves Secure Score (from Defender for Cloud/Microsoft Secure Score), not reduces it. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/defender-for-cloud/secure-score-security-controls

question: To maintain Microsoft support, you must implement the security recommendations provided by Azure Advisor within 30 days.
explanation: There is no support requirement that forces you to apply Advisor recommendations within 30 days. Advisor guides best practices, but does not impose support deadlines.

question: An Azure virtual machine can be in multiple resource groups.
explanation: A resource can only belong to one resource group at a time; it cannot be in multiple resource groups. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/overview

question: A resource group can contain resources from multiple Azure regions.
explanation: Resource groups are logical containers and can include resources deployed in different Azure regions.

prompt: Match each item with its definition.
right: An organization that defines international standards across all industries.
An organization that defines standards used by the U.S. government.
European policy that regulates privacy and data protection.
Public cloud dedicated to U.S. federal and state agencies.

answer.ISO: An organization that defines international standards across all industries.
answer.NIST: An organization that defines standards used by the U.S. government.
answer.GDPR: European policy that regulates privacy and data protection.
answer.Azure Government: Public cloud dedicated to U.S. federal and state agencies.

explanation: ISO defines international standards; NIST defines standards used by the U.S. government; GDPR regulates privacy in the EU; Azure Government is a public cloud dedicated to U.S. government agencies.

question: From which service can you see which user shut down a specific virtual machine during the last 14 days?
explanation: The Activity Log records control‑plane operations (start/stop/delete, etc.) on resources for the last 90 days, including who performed the action. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log

question: A single Microsoft account can be used to manage multiple Azure subscriptions.
explanation: A single identity can have roles across multiple subscriptions within the same tenant or across tenants, managing resources according to RBAC permissions.

question: Two Azure subscriptions can be merged into a single subscription by creating a support request.
explanation: There is no subscription 'merge' operation. You can move compatible resources between subscriptions, but you cannot merge subscriptions. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/move-resource-group-and-subscription

question: A company can use resources from multiple subscriptions.
explanation: It is common to operate multiple subscriptions (by environment, department, isolation) and access across them with proper connectivity and permissions.

question: Creating additional resource groups in an Azure subscription incurs additional costs.
explanation: Resource groups are logical containers with no cost. Charges come from the resources inside the group.

question: Copying several gigabits of data to Azure from an on-premises network through a VPN incurs additional Azure data transfer costs.
explanation: Inbound traffic to Azure is generally not billed; egress is. Provider VPN/ExpressRoute charges may apply, but not Azure ingress.

question: Copying several gigabytes of data from Azure to an on-premises network through a VPN incurs additional Azure data transfer costs.
explanation: Outbound (egress) traffic from Azure incurs cost depending on region and connection type (Internet/ExpressRoute).

prompt: Match each Azure service with its description.
right: Provides the platform for serverless code.
Big data analytics service for machine learning.
Detects and diagnoses anomalies in web applications.

answer.Azure Functions: Provides the platform for serverless code.
answer.Azure Databricks: Big data analytics service for machine learning.
answer.Azure Application Insights: Detects and diagnoses anomalies in web applications.

explanation: Functions runs serverless code; Databricks provides big data analytics and ML; Application Insights monitors and detects anomalies in apps; App Service hosts managed web applications.

question: When you need to delegate permissions to multiple Azure virtual machines simultaneously, you must deploy the virtual machines…
options: using the same Azure Resource Manager template
explanation: Assigning RBAC at the resource group grants permissions over all resources in the group, enabling simultaneous delegation.

question: Monitor threats by using sensors
options: Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) Identity Protection
explanation: ATP (now Microsoft Defender for Identity) uses sensors on domain controllers to detect identity/on‑prem threats. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/defender-for-identity/

question: Apply Azure MFA based on a condition
options: Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) Identity Protection
answer: Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) Identity Protection
explanation: Identity Protection and Conditional Access allow enforcing MFA based on conditions (sign‑in risk, location, device).

prompt: Match the service to the functionality.
right: Online digital assistant that provides voice support.
Uses prior training to predict outcomes with high probability.
Processes data from millions of sensors.

answer.Azure Bot Services: Online digital assistant that provides voice support.
answer.Azure Machine Learning: Uses prior training to predict outcomes with high probability.
answer.Azure IoT Hub: Processes data from millions of sensors.

explanation: Bot Services creates conversational assistants; ML trains and predicts; Functions provides serverless compute; IoT Hub ingests/manages IoT device data at scale.

question: Adding resource groups in an Azure subscription causes additional costs.
explanation: Resource groups have no direct cost; charges come from the resources they contain.

question: Copying 10 GB of data to Azure from an on-premises network through a VPN incurs additional Azure data transfer costs.
explanation: Data ingress to Azure is not billed by Azure; provider connectivity charges may apply.

question: Copying 10 GB of data from Azure to an on-premises network through a VPN incurs additional Azure data transfer costs.
explanation: Egress from Azure has a cost, regardless of whether it occurs via VPN or the Internet.

question: Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) requires the deployment of domain controllers on Azure virtual machines.
explanation: Azure AD is a managed cloud directory service; it does not require domain controllers on VMs. For managed AD DS, use Azure AD DS.

question: Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) provides authentication services for resources hosted in Azure and Microsoft 365.
explanation: Azure AD is the identity and access service that authenticates users/apps for Azure, Microsoft 365, and associated services.

question: Each user account in Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) can be assigned only one license.
explanation: A user can have multiple licenses/plans (e.g., M365, EMS, AAD Premium); they are not limited to a single license.

question: When planning to migrate a public website to Azure, you should plan to…
options: pay to transfer all site data to Azure
reduce the number of connections to the site
explanation: Pay‑as‑you‑go model: you incur monthly costs for compute, storage, and data egress according to the site's usage.

question: A Platform as a Service (PaaS) solution that hosts web applications in Azure provides full control over the operating systems that host the applications.
explanation: In PaaS the operating system is managed by the provider; the user controls the app and configuration, not the underlying OS.

question: A Platform as a Service (PaaS) solution that hosts web applications in Azure provides the ability to scale the platform automatically.
explanation: PaaS services (such as App Service) support automatic scaling based on rules/metrics, reducing administration.

question: A Platform as a Service (PaaS) solution that hosts web applications in Azure provides professional development services to continuously add features to custom applications.
explanation: PaaS provides platform/infrastructure, not professional development services for continuous feature delivery; those are separate.

question: Which tool can calculate cost savings from reduced electricity consumption when migrating on-premises Microsoft SQL servers to Azure?
options: Azure Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) calculator
answer: Azure Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) calculator
explanation: The TCO calculator estimates total costs including energy savings when migrating to Azure, compared to on‑premises. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/tco/calculator/

question: When implementing a Software as a Service (SaaS) solution, you are responsible for…
explanation: In SaaS the provider manages the infrastructure and the application; the customer configures the service (users, options) according to needs.

question: To achieve a hybrid cloud model, a company must always migrate from a private cloud model.
explanation: A hybrid model combines on‑prem and public/private cloud; it does not require migrating from a private cloud and may start from traditional on‑premises.

question: A company can extend the capacity of its internal network by using the public cloud.
explanation: The public cloud allows scaling capacity (compute, storage) connected via VPN/ExpressRoute to extend the internal environment.

question: In a public cloud model, only guest users from your company can access cloud resources.
explanation: Resources can be accessed by authorized internal users, external guests, and/or customers according to permissions; it is not limited to guests.

question: The guaranteed uptime Service Level Agreement (SLA) for paid Azure services is at least 99.9 percent.
explanation: Many services offer ≥99.9% SLA; some are higher (99.95/99.99). Always review the specific SLA per service. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/sla/

question: Companies can increase the guaranteed uptime Service Level Agreement (SLA) by adding Azure resources to multiple regions.
explanation: Multi‑region designs improve composite availability by avoiding regional failures, increasing the effective SLA of the solution.

question: Companies can increase the guaranteed uptime Service Level Agreement (SLA) by purchasing multiple subscriptions.
explanation: SLA depends on the service and architecture (zones/regions); multiple subscriptions do not increase the guaranteed SLA.

question: Identities stored in an on-premises Active Directory can be synchronized with Azure Active Directory (Azure AD).
explanation: Azure AD Connect synchronizes on‑premises AD identities with Azure AD for SSO/hybrid.

question: Identities stored in Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), third-party cloud services, and on-premises Active Directory can be used to access Azure resources.
explanation: Azure supports external identities (B2B, federation) and on‑prem identities to access resources with RBAC/roles.

question: Azure has built-in authentication and authorization services that provide secure access to Azure resources.
explanation: Azure AD, RBAC, and Resource Manager provide built‑in authentication and authorization for secure access.

question: To implement an Azure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) solution, you must implement a federation solution or synchronize on-premises identities to the cloud.
explanation: MFA can be enabled directly in Azure AD (Conditional Access) without federation or synchronization.

question: Two valid methods for Azure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) are image identification and a passport number.
explanation: Valid methods include Authenticator app, SMS, phone call, FIDO2, etc. Image/passport number are not accepted factors.

question: Azure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) can be required for administrative and non-administrative user accounts.
explanation: Using Conditional Access, MFA can be applied to any user group (admins and non‑admins).

question: All Azure services in public preview must be accessed using a separate Azure portal.
explanation: Services in public preview are managed in the same Azure Portal (marked as Preview); they do not require a separate portal.

question: Azure services in public preview can be used in production.
explanation: They can be used in production at the customer's discretion, although they have no SLA and may change.

question: Azure services in public preview are subject to a Service Level Agreement (SLA).
explanation: Features/services in preview are generally excluded from SLAs.

question: An organization that hosts its infrastructure… no longer requires a datacenter.
explanation: Hosting infrastructure in a public cloud removes the need to operate your own datacenter; the provider manages the infrastructure.

question: Monitor the health of Azure services
explanation: Azure Monitor and Service Health show metrics, incidents, and service maintenance.

question: Explore available virtual machine images
explanation: Azure Marketplace offers images and solutions published by Microsoft and partners.

question: View security recommendations
explanation: Azure Advisor and Defender for Cloud provide security recommendations and best practices.

question: Minimum number of virtual machines to meet high availability
explanation: At least two instances distributed (availability set or zones) for fault tolerance.

question: Minimum number of availability zones to meet high availability
explanation: Distributing across two zones within a region provides high availability against a datacenter failure.

prompt: Match the tool to its description.
right: Tool that provides guidance and recommendations to improve an Azure environment.
Set of simplified services to build Artificial Intelligence (AI) applications.

answer.Azure Advisor: Tool that provides guidance and recommendations to improve an Azure environment.
answer.Azure Cognitive Services: Set of simplified services to build Artificial Intelligence (AI) applications.

explanation: DevOps integrates CI/CD and management; Advisor provides recommendations; Cognitive Services offers AI APIs; Application Insights monitors web applications.

prompt: Match IoT services with their description.
right: Managed service that provides bidirectional communication between IoT devices and Azure.
Fully-managed SaaS solution to connect, monitor, and manage IoT devices at scale.
Software and hardware solution that provides communication and security features for IoT devices.

answer.IoT Hub: Managed service that provides bidirectional communication between IoT devices and Azure.
answer.IoT Central: Fully-managed SaaS solution to connect, monitor, and manage IoT devices at scale.
answer.Azure Sphere: Software and hardware solution that provides communication and security features for IoT devices.

explanation: IoT Hub manages device‑to‑cloud messaging; IoT Central is IoT SaaS; Azure Sphere combines hardware/OS/service for security.

question: Azure virtual machines are an example of…
explanation: Virtual machines are infrastructure managed by the customer (compute, network, and storage) and therefore IaaS.

question: Azure SQL Database is an example of…
explanation: Azure SQL Database is a managed database (updates, high availability, backups) delivered as PaaS.

question: To prevent a VM named VM1 from connecting to other VMs in the environment, VM1 must…
options: be in a separate virtual network
be in a separate resource group
answer: be in a separate virtual network
explanation: Isolating VM1 in a separate VNet (without peering) removes internal routes and prevents connectivity with other VMs. This can also be reinforced with NSGs.

question: If a resource group RG1 has a Delete lock, who can delete RG1?
options: only a member of the global administrators group
the Delete lock must be removed before an administrator can delete RG1
an Azure policy must be modified before an administrator can delete RG1
an Azure tag must be added before an administrator can delete RG1
answer: the Delete lock must be removed before an administrator can delete RG1
explanation: A Delete lock prevents deletion until the lock is removed on the resource/group/subscription.

question: Your company implements ____ to automatically add a watermark to Word documents that contain credit card information.
explanation: Azure Information Protection classifies/labels content and can apply watermarks based on rules (e.g., credit‑card detection).

question: An Azure web app that queries an on-premises Microsoft SQL server is an example of a ____ cloud.
explanation: Combines cloud resources (web app in Azure) with on‑prem resources (SQL Server), forming a hybrid model.

question: Azure China is operated by Microsoft.
explanation: Azure China is operated by 21Vianet in compliance with local regulations; not directly by Microsoft.

question: Azure Government is operated by Microsoft.
explanation: Azure Government is an isolated environment operated by Microsoft for U.S. government workloads.

question: Azure Government is available only for U.S. government agencies and their partners.
explanation: Access to Azure Government is restricted to U.S. government agencies and authorized partners.

question: An Azure resource can have multiple Delete locks.
explanation: Locks are inherited and their effect is consolidated; multiple Delete locks do not change behavior—one is sufficient to prevent deletion.

question: An Azure resource inherits the locks from its resource group.
explanation: Locks applied at subscription or resource group scope propagate to contained resources (lock inheritance).

question: If an Azure resource has a Read-only lock, you can add a Delete lock to the resource.
explanation: With a read‑only lock, the resource cannot be modified or deleted; adding an additional Delete lock is unnecessary.

question: Most Azure services are introduced in private preview before being introduced in public preview and then in general availability.
explanation: Typical lifecycle: Private Preview → Public Preview → General Availability (GA).

question: Azure services in public preview can only be managed by Azure CLI.
explanation: Preview services are usually available via Azure Portal and APIs, not only CLI.

question: The cost of an Azure service in private preview decreases when the service becomes generally available.
explanation: There is no rule that prices decrease when reaching GA; pricing is set per service and may change.

question: All Azure resources deployed in a resource group must use the same Azure region.
explanation: A resource group is a logical container; it can contain resources in different regions (depending on the service).

question: If you assign a tag to a resource group, all Azure resources in that resource group will be assigned the same tag.
explanation: Tags are not inherited automatically; they can be enforced/propagated with Azure Policy if desired.

question: If you assign permissions to a user to manage a resource group, the user can manage all Azure resources in that resource group.
explanation: RBAC permissions assigned at the resource group apply to contained resources, unless there are more restrictive assignments.

question: In Azure Active Directory Premium P1, at least 99.9 percent availability is guaranteed.
explanation: Azure AD Premium has a 99.9% availability SLA for authentication and directory services. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/sla/active-directory/

question: The Service Level Agreement (SLA) for Azure Active Directory Premium P1 is the same as the SLA for Azure Active Directory Free.
explanation: Premium editions have a formal SLA; the Free edition does not offer the same SLA. See SLA details per edition. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/sla/active-directory/

question: For pay-as-you-go accounts, the monthly credit is 10% if the uptime percentage is less than 99.9%.
explanation: SLA credits apply per service according to SLA metrics; there is no generic 10% credit for a PAYG account. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/sla/

question: A user with the Owner role can transfer the ownership of an Azure subscription.
explanation: The Owner role (RBAC) manages resources, not subscription ownership. Changing the subscription owner requires account/billing permissions (e.g., Account Owner). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/change-azure-subscription-owner

question: You can convert your company's Azure subscription from Free Trial to Pay-As-You-Go.
explanation: After the free trial ends, you can upgrade to Pay‑As‑You‑Go to continue using services without the spending limit. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/spending-limit#remove-the-spending-limit

question: The Azure spending limit is fixed and cannot be increased or decreased.
explanation: The spending limit can be removed or re‑enabled on eligible accounts; it is not immutable. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/spending-limit

question: With Azure Reservations, you pay less for virtual machines than with pay-as-you-go pricing.
explanation: Reservations (1/3 years) offer significant discounts compared to PAYG by committing capacity. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/save-compute-costs-reservations

question: If you create two Azure virtual machines using size B2S, each virtual machine will always incur the same monthly costs.
explanation: Cost varies by region, OS/licensing, runtime hours, disks, and traffic. Same size does not guarantee the same monthly cost.

question: When an Azure virtual machine is stopped, you still pay for the storage costs associated with the virtual machine.
explanation: When stopped (deallocated) compute is not billed, but disks (managed/unmanaged) and snapshots continue to incur costs.

question: If your company uses an Azure free account, you can only use a subset of Azure services.
explanation: The free account limits available services/resources and quotas; not the entire catalog is included. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/free/

question: All Azure free accounts expire after a specific period.
explanation: The free trial expires after the promotional period (e.g., 30 days). You must upgrade to a paid offer to continue.

question: You can create up to 10 Azure free accounts using the same Microsoft account.
explanation: La oferta gratuita está limitada a nuevos clientes y no permite múltiples cuentas gratuitas con el mismo inicio de sesión.

question: Azure Monitor can monitor the performance of on-premises computers.
explanation: Mediante agentes (Azure Monitor Agent/Log Analytics) se recopilan métricas y logs desde equipos on-prem. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/agents/agents-overview

question: Azure Monitor can send alerts to Azure Active Directory security groups.
explanation: Los grupos de acciones pueden enviar correo a direcciones de grupo (Azure AD), webhooks, SMS, etc. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/action-groups

question: Azure Monitor can trigger alerts based on data in an Azure Log Analytics workspace.
explanation: Las alertas de Log Analytics usan consultas KQL sobre workspaces para evaluar condiciones y activar grupos de acciones. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-log

question: The document that explains which data Microsoft processes, how it is processed, and the purpose of the processing is:
options: Online Subscription Agreement for Microsoft Azure
explanation: La Declaración de privacidad describe qué datos procesa Microsoft, cómo y con qué propósito. Los Términos y SLA cubren condiciones de servicio y disponibilidad.

question: Azure offers flexibility between capital expenditure (CapEx) and operational expenditure (OpEx).
explanation: La nube permite pasar de CapEx (hardware propio) a OpEx (pago por uso), flexibilizando el modelo financiero.

prompt: Match the cloud service with its characteristic.
left: A cloud service that remains available after a failure occurs
A cloud service that can recover after a failure occurs
A cloud service that responds quickly as load increases
A cloud service that can be accessed quickly from the Internet
explanation: Tolerancia a fallos mantiene servicio pese a fallas; recuperación ante desastres restaura servicio tras fallas; escalabilidad dinámica ajusta capacidad rápidamente; baja latencia reduce tiempos de acceso.

question: Your Azure trial account expired last week. Now you cannot…
options: create additional Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) user accounts
start an existing Azure virtual machine
access your data stored in Azure
answer: start an existing Azure virtual machine
explanation: Al expirar la prueba, los recursos se deshabilitan hasta actualizar a una oferta de pago; no se pueden iniciar VMs. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/spending-limit#what-happens-when-i-hit-my-spending-limit

question: You assign the Azure Policy definition 'Not Allowed Resource Type' and specify that virtual networks are not allowed in RG1. With an existing virtual network VNET1 in RG1, VNET1…
options: is automatically moved to another resource group
explanation: La política Deny impide nuevas creaciones del tipo, pero no afecta retroactivamente a recursos existentes a menos que se aplique remediación específica. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure

question: An Azure Policy initiative is…
options: a collection of Azure Policy definition assignments
a group of role assignments for role-based access control (RBAC)
a group of Azure Blueprints definitions
explanation: Una iniciativa agrupa múltiples definiciones de política para asignarlas juntas y medir cumplimiento agregado. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/policy/concepts/initiative-definition

question: You can use Availability Zones in Azure to protect virtual machines from a datacenter failure.
explanation: Las zonas de disponibilidad son datacenters independientes dentro de una región; desplegar VMs en distintas zonas protege ante fallo de un datacenter. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/reliability/availability-zones-overview

question: You can use Availability Zones in Azure to protect virtual machines from a region failure.
explanation: Las zonas están dentro de una región; para fallo de región se usa redundancia entre regiones (pares de región, multirregión).

question: You can use Availability Zones in Azure to protect Azure managed disks from a datacenter failure.
explanation: Los discos con redundancia de zona (ZRS para Managed Disks, disponible en regiones soportadas) ofrecen resiliencia frente fallo de datacenter. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/disks-redundancy

question: An Azure subscription can be associated with multiple Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenants.
explanation: Una suscripción pertenece a un único inquilino (tenant) de Azure AD a la vez.

question: You can change the Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenant associated to an Azure subscription.
explanation: Se puede mover la suscripción a otro directorio (cambiar tenant) siguiendo los requisitos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/change-directory

question: When an Azure subscription expires, the associated Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenant is automatically deleted.
explanation: El tenant de Azure AD no se elimina automáticamente al expirar la suscripción; puede mantenerse para otros usos/licencias.

question: Azure resources can only access other resources in the same resource group.
explanation: Los recursos pueden comunicarse entre grupos y suscripciones si hay conectividad/red y permisos adecuados.

question: If you delete a resource group, all resources in the group will be deleted.
explanation: Eliminar el grupo de recursos borra todos los recursos contenidos de forma irreversible (salvo recursos bloqueados).

question: Building a datacenter infrastructure is an example of operational expenditure (OpEx) costs.
explanation: Construir infraestructura es CapEx (inversión en capital), no gasto operativo.

question: Monthly salaries of technical staff are an example of operational expenditure (OpEx) costs.
explanation: Salarios mensuales son gastos operativos recurrentes (OpEx).

question: Leasing software is an example of operational expenditure (OpEx) costs.
explanation: El alquiler/licenciamiento por suscripción de software es OpEx (pago recurrente por uso).

question: With a consumption-based plan, you pay a fixed rate for all data sent to or from cloud-hosted virtual machines.
explanation: Los costes de datos dependen de dirección (in/out), región, servicio y plan; no es una tarifa fija universal.

question: With a consumption-based plan, you reduce costs by paying only for extra capacity when required.
explanation: Modelo de consumo permite escalar y pagar solo por recursos utilizados cuando se requieren.

question: Serverless computing is an example of a consumption-based plan.
explanation: Serverless factura por ejecución y consumo (tiempo/memoria), alineado con modelo bajo demanda.

question: Provides organizations the ability to manage Azure resource compliance across multiple subscriptions.
explanation: Azure Policy evalúa y aplica reglas de cumplimiento a escala (suscripciones/grupos de administración).

question: You have several virtual machines in an Azure subscription. You create a new subscription. What happens to the virtual machines?
options: The virtual machines cannot be moved to the new subscription
The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription
The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription only if they are all in the same resource group
The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription only if they run Windows Server 2016
answer: The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription
explanation: Se pueden mover recursos entre suscripciones (move operation) con ciertas limitaciones por tipo/región.

question: After creating a virtual machine, you must modify the ____ to allow connections to TCP port 8080 on the virtual machine.
explanation: Los NSG definen reglas de seguridad (puertos/protocolos) para permitir tráfico entrante/saliente como TCP 8080.

question: An Azure service is available to all Azure customers when it is in…
explanation: En Public Preview los servicios están abiertos a todos los clientes (con limitaciones); Private Preview es por invitación.

question: Availability Zones can be implemented in all Azure regions.
explanation: Las zonas de disponibilidad sólo existen en regiones compatibles; no están disponibles en todas las regiones.

question: Only virtual machines that run Windows Server can be created in Availability Zones.
explanation: Las zonas no restringen el SO; se pueden crear VMs Linux/Windows en zonas según soporte regional.

question: Availability Zones are used to replicate data and applications to multiple regions.
explanation: Las zonas protegen dentro de una región; para replicación entre regiones se usan pares/multirregión.

question: All Azure services that are in public preview are…
options: configurable only from the Azure portal
explanation: Los servicios en preview generalmente no están cubiertos por SLA; la documentación/gestión varía por servicio.

question: From the Azure portal, you can distinguish between services in general availability (GA) and services in public preview.
explanation: El portal identifica características en preview con etiquetas ‘Preview’; GA no muestra ese estado.

question: When an Azure service reaches general availability (GA), it is no longer in preview.
explanation: GA indica disponibilidad general; por definición deja de ser ‘preview’.

question: When you create resources for a service in public preview, you must recreate them when the service becomes generally available (GA).
explanation: Normalmente no se requiere recrear recursos al pasar a GA; pueden cambiar APIs/propiedades, pero se migra sin recreación en la mayoría de casos.

question: Storing 1 TB of data in Azure Blob Storage always costs the same regardless of the Azure region in which the data resides.
explanation: Los precios de Blob varían por región, nivel de acceso (Hot/Cool/Archive) y redundancia (LRS/ZRS/GRS).

question: In a General Purpose v2 Azure Storage account, you are charged only for the amount of data stored; all read and write operations are free.
explanation: Además del almacenamiento, se cobran operaciones/solicitudes (lecturas/escrituras), ancho de banda y otros conceptos.

question: Transferring data between Azure Storage accounts in different Azure regions is free.
explanation: Las transferencias entre regiones incurren en cargos de egreso y posibles costos de replicación.

question: Using an Azure ExpressRoute connection, incoming data traffic from an on-premises network to Azure is always free.
explanation: El tráfico entrante por ExpressRoute no tiene cargo por GB; se paga el circuito y el puerto.

question: Outgoing data traffic from Azure to an on-premises network is always free.
explanation: El tráfico saliente desde Azure tiene cargos; con ExpressRoute depende del plan (metered/unlimited).

question: Data traffic between Azure services within the same Azure region is always free.
explanation: Generalmente el tráfico intra-región entre servicios de Azure es gratuito; se cobran transferencias entre regiones o hacia Internet.

question: Each Azure subscription can contain multiple account administrators.
explanation: El rol de Account/Billing Administrator es único; la administración de recursos usa RBAC con varios roles, no múltiples 'account admins'.

question: Each Azure subscription can only be managed using a Microsoft account.
explanation: Las suscripciones se administran también con cuentas organizacionales (Azure AD); no sólo cuentas Microsoft (MSA).

question: You can enable Just-In-Time (JIT) access to virtual machines by using…
explanation: Defender for Cloud (antes Security Center) habilita JIT para VMs, abriendo puertos sólo en ventanas aprobadas.

question: To implement a hybrid cloud model, a company must have an internal network.
explanation: Un modelo híbrido implica recursos locales conectados (red interna/VPN/ExpressRoute) junto con servicios en la nube.

question: An Azure Virtual Desktop (Windows Virtual Desktop) session host can only run Windows 10.
explanation: AVD soporta Windows 10/11 multi-session y Windows Server como sistemas operativos de hosts de sesión.

question: An Azure Virtual Desktop host pool that includes 25 session hosts supports a maximum of 20 simultaneous user connections.
explanation: La capacidad depende del tamaño y rendimiento de los hosts; no existe un límite fijo de 20 conexiones.

question: Azure Virtual Desktop supports desktop and application virtualization.
explanation: Permite publicar escritorios completos y aplicaciones remotas (RemoteApp) para usuarios finales.

question: Data copied to an Azure Storage account is automatically kept with at least three copies.
explanation: La redundancia LRS mantiene tres copias dentro del mismo datacenter; opciones ZRS/GRS añaden replicación adicional.

question: All data copied to an Azure Storage account is automatically backed up in another Azure datacenter.
explanation: Sólo cuentas con GRS/GZRS replican a una segunda región; no es automático en todas las cuentas.

question: An Azure Storage account can contain up to 7 TB of data and up to one million files.
explanation: Los objetivos de escala por cuenta superan ampliamente 7 TB (múltiples PiB según tipo).

question:
Your company plans to migrate all its data and resources to Azure.

The company's migration plan states that only Platform as a Service (PaaS) solutions must be used in Azure. You need to deploy an Azure environment that meets the company migration plan.

Solution: You create an Azure App Service and Azure SQL databases.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Ambos servicios (App Service y Azure SQL Database) son PaaS y cumplen el objetivo.

question:
You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines.

You need to ensure that the services running on the virtual machines are available if a single datacenter fails.

Solution: You deploy the virtual machines to two or more resource groups.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: El uso de varios resource groups no proporciona resiliencia de datacenter; se deben usar Availability Zones o conjuntos de disponibilidad.

question:
If Microsoft plans to end support for an Azure service that does NOT have a successor service, Microsoft will provide notification at least __ before.

(Evaluate the underlined text: 12 months.)
explanation: Microsoft proporciona notificación con al menos 12 meses cuando retira un servicio sin sucesor, para permitir planificación de migración. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/lifecycle/policies/azure

question: You need to allow a development team to start and stop Azure virtual machines only on specific occasions, using least privilege and minimizing costs. Which security feature should you use?
explanation: PIM habilita asignaciones temporales de roles con menor privilegio y aprobación (JIT), ajustándose a ventanas específicas para arrancar/parar VMs minimizando costes y riesgos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/privileged-identity-management/

question:
Your company has an Azure subscription that contains the following unused resources: 20 user accounts in Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), five groups in Azure AD, 10 public IP addresses, and 10 network interfaces.

You need to reduce the Azure costs for the company.

Solution: You remove the unused network interfaces.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Eliminar NICs sin uso no suele impactar significativamente en costes. Los principales costes provienen de VMs, discos, direcciones IP públicas, etc. Para reducir coste, enfoque en recursos facturables.

question: When planning to migrate a public website to Azure, /underline you must plan to pay monthly usage costs. /underline/ (Evaluate the underlined text.)
options: Pay to transfer all the website data to Azure
Reduce the number of connections to the website
explanation: La nube tiene modelo de pago por uso (compute, storage, red). Es correcto planificar costes mensuales según consumo del sitio.

question:
A company needs an automated process to upload logs to an Azure SQL database every week, and then generate reports from the SQL database.

Which service should you use?
explanation: ADF programa y orquesta pipelines de ingestión y transformación hacia Azure SQL Database, ideal para cargas semanales y reporting.

question:
You need to ensure that when Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) users connect to Azure AD from the internet using an anonymous IP address, the users are prompted automatically to change their password.

Which Azure service should you use?
explanation: Identity Protection detecta inicios de sesión de riesgo (por ejemplo, IP anónima) y puede exigir acciones como cambio de contraseña mediante políticas. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/identity-protection/overview-identity-protection

question:
Your company hosts an accounting application named App1 that is used by all the customers. App1 has low usage during the first three weeks of each month and very high usage during the last week of each month.

Which benefit of Azure Cloud Services supports cost management for this type of usage pattern?
explanation: La elasticidad permite ajustar capacidad según demanda (escalar/descender) y pagar solo por lo que se usa, optimizando costes.

question: On Windows, Command Prompt can be used to run the Azure CLI.
explanation: En Windows, el instalador MSI del Azure CLI añade el comando `az` al PATH, por lo que puede ejecutarse desde Command Prompt (cmd.exe) y también desde PowerShell. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/cli/azure/install-azure-cli-windows

question: On Windows, Windows PowerShell can be used to run the Azure CLI.
explanation: Azure CLI es multiplataforma. En Windows se puede ejecutar `az` desde PowerShell además de CMD. También está disponible en Azure Cloud Shell. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/cli/azure/

question:
Your company has several business units. Each business unit requires 20 different Azure resources for daily operation, and all units require the same type of Azure resources.

You need a solution to automate the creation of the Azure resources.

What should you recommend?
explanation: Las plantillas ARM permiten definir la infraestructura como código (IaC) y desplegar conjuntos de recursos de forma repetible y consistente para múltiples unidades de negocio. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/templates/overview

question:
The Azure /underline Standard /underline/ support plan is the lowest cost option to receive 24x7 access to support engineers by phone.

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
explanation: El plan Developer no incluye soporte 24x7 por teléfono; el plan Standard sí ofrece acceso 24x7 a ingenieros por teléfono/correo para incidencias críticas y es el nivel más económico que lo incluye. Referencia: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/plans/

question:
You have an Azure environment.

You need to create a new Azure virtual machine from a tablet that runs the Android operating system.

Solution: You use the Azure portal.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: El portal de Azure es una aplicación web accesible desde navegadores modernos, incluido en tablets Android. Desde el portal se pueden crear y administrar máquinas virtuales. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-portal/azure-portal-overview

question:
You have a virtual machine named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2016 in the East US region.

Which Azure service should you use from the Azure portal to view service failure notifications that can affect the availability of VM1?
explanation: En la página de 'Máquinas virtuales' del portal existe la columna 'Estado de mantenimiento' que muestra incidencias del host que podrían afectar a la VM. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/maintenance-and-updates

question:
You need to create VM1 in Subscription1 by using the command

'az vm create --resource-group RG1 --name VM1 --image UbuntuLTS --generate-ssh-keys'.

Solution: From the Azure portal, launch Azure Cloud Shell and select PowerShell.

Run the command in Cloud Shell. Does this meet the goal?
explanation: El comando de Azure CLI puede ejecutarse en Azure Cloud Shell. Aunque selecciones PowerShell en Cloud Shell, los comandos 'az' funcionan tanto en PowerShell como en Bash. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/linux/quick-create-cli

question: You plan to provision Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) resources in Azure. Which resource is an example of IaaS?
explanation: Una máquina virtual de Azure es un ejemplo de IaaS. Los servicios Azure Web App, Azure Logic Apps y Azure SQL Database son ejemplos de PaaS. Referencias: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/overview/what-is-iaas/ y https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/overview/what-is-paas/

question: To ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP, modifying a network security group (NSG) is a valid solution.
explanation: Un NSG funciona como firewall de capa 4 y puede asociarse a una subred o a la NIC de la VM. Agregar una regla de entrada para el puerto 80 (HTTP) permite el acceso desde Internet. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/security-overview

question: To ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP, modifying Azure Firewall is a valid solution.
explanation: Azure Firewall puede controlar y permitir tráfico hacia recursos de Azure. Configurar una regla para permitir HTTP puede cumplir el objetivo dependiendo de la arquitectura de red. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/firewall/overview

question: What should you use to evaluate whether your company's Azure environment meets regulatory requirements?
options: the Advisor blade from the Azure portal
Compliance Manager from the Service Trust Portal
the Solutions blade from the Azure portal
answer: Compliance Manager from the Service Trust Portal
explanation: Compliance Manager en el Service Trust Portal ayuda a rastrear, asignar y verificar actividades de cumplimiento normativo para servicios de Microsoft Cloud como Azure y Microsoft 365. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/microsoft-365/compliance/get-started-with-service-trust-portal?view=o365-worldwide

question:
You need to create VM1 in Subscription1 by using the command

'az vm create --resource-group RG1 --name VM1 --image UbuntuLTS --generate-ssh-keys'.

Solution: From a computer that runs Windows 10, install Azure CLI. From PowerShell, sign in to Azure and then run the command. Does this meet the goal?
explanation: El comando de Azure CLI puede ejecutarse desde PowerShell o el símbolo del sistema si Azure CLI está instalado. Sin embargo, debe ejecutarse en el equipo Windows 10, no desde Azure Portal. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/cli/azure/install-azure-cli-windows?view=azure-cli-latest

question:
You have an on-premises network that contains 100 servers. You need to recommend a solution that provides additional resources to your users.

The solution must minimize capital and operational expenditure costs.

What should you include in the recommendation?
options: a complete migration to the public cloud
explanation: Una nube híbrida combina nube privada y pública. Puedes seguir usando servidores locales mientras añades nuevos servidores en Azure, lo que minimiza el CapEx (no compras hardware nuevo) y optimiza el OpEx. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/principles-cloud-computing/4-cloud-deployment-models

question: Which service provides serverless computing in Azure?
explanation: Azure Functions proporciona una plataforma de ejecución serverless basada en eventos sin administrar infraestructura. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/

question: You have an Azure web app. From an iPhone, you can manage the settings of the web app by using the Azure portal.
explanation: El portal de Azure es basado en web y accesible desde navegadores móviles (incluido iPhone), por lo que permite administrar apps web. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-portal/azure-portal-overview

question: You have an Azure web app. From an iPhone, you can manage the settings of the web app by using Azure Cloud Shell.
explanation: Azure Cloud Shell es una consola basada en web integrada en el portal; al ser web, se puede usar desde iPhone para gestionar recursos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-shell/overview

question:
An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources.

Solution: Run the script from a computer that runs Windows 10 and has the Azure PowerShell module installed.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Un script de PowerShell debe ejecutarse en PowerShell con el módulo de Azure instalado (Az). En Windows 10 con Azure PowerShell, la solución cumple el objetivo. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/azure/overview?view=azps-11.3.0

question:
An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources.

Solution: Run the script from a computer that runs Linux and has the Azure CLI tools installed.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: El script requiere PowerShell. Tener solo las herramientas de Azure CLI en Linux no proporciona el entorno de PowerShell necesario para ejecutar el script. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/overview?view=powershell-7.4

question: After migrating servers to Azure virtual machines, replacing failed server hardware will no longer be your administrative responsibility.
explanation: Las VM de Azure se ejecutan sobre servidores físicos Hyper‑V administrados por Microsoft. El reemplazo de hardware averiado lo gestiona Microsoft; el cliente no tiene acceso al hardware físico. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/overview

question: After migrating servers to Azure virtual machines, managing physical server security will no longer be your administrative responsibility.
explanation: La seguridad física de los servidores de Azure es responsabilidad de Microsoft. Al mover cargas a VM de Azure se elimina la gestión de seguridad física del hardware por parte del cliente. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/security/fundamentals/physical-security

question:
You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that the services running on the virtual machines are available if a single data center fails.

Solution: You deploy the virtual machines to a scale set.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Los scale sets ofrecen alta disponibilidad y escalado, pero para resistir la caída de un datacenter deben desplegarse a través de Availability Zones o regiones emparejadas. No se especifica que el scale set abarque múltiples centros. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machine-scale-sets/availability

question:
/underline Authorization /underline/ is the process of verifying a user's credentials.

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
explanation: Verificar credenciales corresponde a autenticación. La autorización concede permisos a recursos tras autenticarse. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/authentication-vs-authorization

question: Which task can you perform by using Azure Advisor?
options: Integrate Active Directory and Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)
Estimate the costs of an Azure solution
Confirm that Azure subscription security follows best practices
Evaluate which on-premises resources can be migrated to Azure
answer: Estimate the costs of an Azure solution
explanation: Azure Advisor entrega recomendaciones de optimización de costos con estimaciones de ahorro basadas en uso. Para estimar el costo total de una solución nueva se usa Azure Pricing Calculator. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/advisor/advisor-overview

question: A United States government entity is eligible to use Azure Government to develop a cloud solution.
explanation: Azure Government está diseñado para entidades gubernamentales de EE. UU. en todos los niveles. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/learn/modules/intro-to-azure-government/2-what-is-azure-government

question: A United States government contractor is eligible to use Azure Government to develop a cloud solution.
explanation: Azure Government está disponible para agencias gubernamentales de EE. UU. y sus contratistas/partners con cargas de trabajo elegibles. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/learn/modules/intro-to-azure-government/2-what-is-azure-government

question: When designing infrastructure for a stateless microservices app with Azure Service Fabric, you should consider the number of node types in the cluster.
explanation: El dimensionamiento/capacidad del clúster depende del número de tipos de nodo y su configuración. Referencia: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/service-fabric/service-fabric-cluster-capacity

question: When designing infrastructure for a stateless microservices app with Azure Service Fabric, you should consider the properties for each node type.
explanation: Las propiedades del tipo de nodo (recuento de nodos, tamaño, durabilidad, colocation) afectan capacidad y resiliencia del clúster. Referencia: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/service-fabric/service-fabric-cluster-capacity

question:
An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources.

Solution: Run the script from a computer that runs Chrome OS and uses Azure Cloud Shell.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: En Azure Cloud Shell se puede ejecutar PowerShell desde un navegador (incluido Chrome OS) con el módulo de Azure preinstalado. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-shell/quickstart-powershell

question:
You deploy two Azure Virtual Machines in two separate regions and then create an Azure Traffic Manager profile to provide redundancy if an Azure region fails.

Does this meet the requirement?
explanation: Traffic Manager distribuye tráfico DNS entre endpoints en múltiples regiones y puede aplicar failover, cumpliendo requisitos de redundancia geográfica. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/traffic-manager/traffic-manager-overview

question:
You deploy an Azure resource and it becomes unavailable for an extended period due to a service outage.

/underline Microsoft will automatically refund your bank account. /underline/

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
options: automatically migrate the resource to another subscription
send you a coupon code that you can redeem for Azure credits
explanation: Ante incumplimientos de SLA, Microsoft otorga créditos de servicio a la suscripción (no reembolsos bancarios). Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/framework/resiliency/sla

question:
You plan to deploy an Azure Web App to two regions and need the app to keep running if a region fails while minimizing deployment costs.

Which service should you include?
explanation: Traffic Manager ofrece enrutamiento DNS con failover global a bajo coste para aplicaciones en múltiples regiones. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/traffic-manager/traffic-manager-overview

question:
A team of developers plans to deploy, and then remove, 50 virtual machines each week using Azure Resource Manager templates.

Which Azure service will minimize the administrative effort to deploy and remove the virtual machines?
explanation: DevTest Labs permite aprovisionar y retirar rápidamente VMs basadas en plantillas reutilizables, automatizando tareas y controlando costos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devtest-labs/devtest-lab-overview

question:
Administrators must be allowed to create Azure resources only in the region of the country where their office is located.

Which Azure resource should you create to meet this policy requirement?
explanation: Azure Policy puede restringir las ubicaciones permitidas (Allowed locations) para la creación de recursos y cumplir la directiva. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/policy/overview

question:
You will host SQL Server on an Azure virtual machine with two data disks: one for the log file and one for the data files.

Which caching policy should you recommend for the disk containing the logs?
explanation: Para SQL Server, el disco de logs debe usar caché 'None' para garantizar durabilidad y rendimiento de escritura secuencial. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-sql/virtual-machines/windows/performance-best-practices-storage

question:
Your company has 10 departments and plans to implement Azure. You need each department to use a different payment option for the Azure services it consumes.

What should you create for each department?
explanation: La facturación y opciones de pago en Azure se gestionan por suscripción. Crear una suscripción por departamento permite separar costes y métodos de pago. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/create-subscription

question:
/underline Resource groups provide organizations /underline/ with the ability to manage the compliance of Azure resources across multiple subscriptions.

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
explanation: La conformidad de recursos se gestiona con Azure Policy mediante reglas y evaluaciones; los grupos de recursos organizan recursos y no aplican cumplimiento. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/policy/overview

question:
An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources.

Solution: Run the script from a computer that runs macOS and has PowerShell Core 6.0 installed.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: PowerShell Core 6 es multiplataforma y permite ejecutar cmdlets y scripts de PowerShell en macOS. Para crear recursos de Azure, se requiere el módulo Azure PowerShell instalado. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/overview?view=powershell-6

question:
You plan to migrate several servers from an on-premises network to Azure.

What is an advantage of using a public cloud service for the servers over an on-premises network?
options: The public cloud is owned by the public, NOT a private corporation
The public cloud is a crowd-sourcing solution that provides corporations with the ability to enhance the cloud
All public cloud resources can be freely accessed by every member of the public
The public cloud is a shared entity whereby multiple corporations each use a portion of the resources in the cloud
answer: The public cloud is a shared entity whereby multiple corporations each use a portion of the resources in the cloud
explanation: La nube pública es un entorno compartido operado por un proveedor (p. ej., Microsoft) donde múltiples clientes consumen recursos de forma segura y aislada bajo un modelo multi‑tenant. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/cloud-adoption/getting-started/what-is-azure

question:
You plan to deploy 20 virtual machines to Azure. To ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 cannot connect to the other virtual machines, /underline VM1 must be deployed to a separate virtual network. /underline/

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
options: run a different operating system than the other virtual machines
be deployed to a separate resource group
explanation: Para garantizar aislamiento de red, desplegar VM1 en una red virtual distinta evita conectividad directa con VMs en otras vNets salvo emparejamiento o VPN. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview

question:
Your company plans to migrate all its data and resources to Azure. The company's migration plan states that only Platform as a Service (PaaS) solutions must be used in Azure.

Solution: You create Azure virtual machines, Azure SQL databases, and Azure Storage accounts.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Las máquinas virtuales son IaaS, por lo que la solución propuesta no cumple con usar únicamente PaaS. Azure SQL y Storage son PaaS, pero incluir VMs rompe el requisito. Referencias: https://azure.microsoft.com/overview/what-is-paas/ y https://azure.microsoft.com/overview/what-is-iaas/

question:
Your network contains an Active Directory forest with 5,000 user accounts. You plan to migrate all network resources to Azure and decommission the on‑premises datacenter. You need to recommend a solution to minimize the impact on users after the planned migration.

What should you recommend?
options: Sync all the Active Directory user accounts to Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)
Instruct all users to change their password
Create a guest user account in Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) for each user
answer: Sync all the Active Directory user accounts to Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)
explanation: Usar Azure AD Connect para sincronizar identidades y (opcionalmente) contraseñas reduce el impacto, habilita SSO y simplifica la transición. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/hybrid/how-to-connect-sync-whatis

question:
Your company plans to request an architectural review of an Azure environment from Microsoft. The company currently has a Basic support plan. You need to recommend a new support plan that minimizes costs.

Which support plan should you recommend?
explanation: El plan Premier incluye soporte específico de arquitectura (design reviews, tuning y asistencia de implementación) impartido por especialistas técnicos de Microsoft. Developer/Standard no ofrecen revisiones arquitectónicas y Professional Direct no proporciona el mismo nivel. Referencia: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/plans

question:
An Availability Zone in Azure has physically separate locations /underline across two continents. /underline/

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
explanation: Las Availability Zones son ubicaciones físicas únicas dentro de una misma región de Azure, diseñadas para resistir fallos de datacenter. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/availability-zones/az-overview

question:
A team of developers at your company plans to deploy, and then remove, 50 customized virtual machines each week. Thirty run Windows Server 2016 and 20 run Ubuntu Linux.

Which Azure service will minimize the administrative effort required to deploy and remove the virtual machines?
explanation: DevTest Labs permite aprovisionar y retirar rápidamente VMs Windows y Linux basadas en plantillas y artefactos, automatizando tareas y controlando costos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devtest-labs/devtest-lab-overview

question:
/underline Azure Databricks /underline/ is an Apache Spark-based analytics service.

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
explanation: Azure Databricks es una plataforma de análisis basada en Apache Spark con componentes como MLlib y entornos colaborativos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/databricks/what-is-databricks

question:
A support plan solution that gives you best practice information, health status and notifications, and 24/7 access to billing information at the lowest possible cost is a /underline Standard /underline/ support plan.

(Evaluate the underlined text.)
explanation: El plan Basic es el de menor coste e incluye acceso 24/7 a información de facturación, documentación y recomendaciones (Azure Advisor/Service Health). El plan Standard tiene coste superior. Referencia: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/plans

question:
You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that the services running on the virtual machines are available if a single data center fails.

Solution: You deploy the virtual machines to two or more availability zones.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Las Availability Zones son zonas físicas separadas dentro de una región. Desplegar en dos o más zonas proporciona tolerancia a fallos de un datacenter. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/availability-zones/az-overview

question:
Your company has an Azure subscription that contains the following unused resources: 20 user accounts in Azure AD, five groups in Azure AD, 10 public IP addresses, and 10 network interfaces. You need to reduce Azure costs.

Solution: Remove the unused user accounts.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Las cuentas de usuario de Azure AD no generan cargos por sí mismas; eliminarlas no reduce costos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/advisor/advisor-cost-recommendations

question: What is required to use Azure Cost Management?
explanation: Históricamente Cost Management estuvo disponible para clientes con EA. Actualmente también para MCA/MPA, pero la pregunta clásica exige EA. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-overview

question:
You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that the services running on the virtual machines are available if a single datacenter fails.

Solution: You deploy the virtual machines to two or more regions.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Distribuir VMs en dos o más regiones separa cargas en múltiples datacenters, aumentando disponibilidad si falla un datacenter. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/windows/regions

question:
You have an on-premises network that contains several servers. You plan to migrate all the servers to Azure. You need to recommend a solution to ensure that some of the servers are available if a single Azure datacenter goes offline for an extended period.

What should you include in the recommendation?
explanation: La tolerancia a fallos asegura continuidad del servicio ante fallos (p. ej., replicación entre zonas/regiones). Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/windows/manage-availability

question: What is guaranteed in an Azure Service Level Agreement (SLA) for virtual machines?
explanation: El SLA de máquinas virtuales garantiza 'uptime' (Virtual Machine Connectivity). Los porcentajes de disponibilidad dependen de la configuración (una sola instancia, conjunto de disponibilidad, zonas). Referencias: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/sla/summary/ , https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/

question:
Your company plans to purchase an Azure subscription. The company's support policy states that Azure Environment must provide an option to access support engineers by phone or email.

You need to recommend a Basic support plan.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: El plan Basic no incluye acceso a ingenieros de soporte por teléfono o correo. Para soporte técnico se requieren Standard, Professional Direct o Premier. Referencia: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/plans/

question:
A company is planning on deploying an application onto Azure. The application will be based on the .NET Core programming language and hosted using Azure Web Apps. One requirement is to help business analyze how many users actually return to the application.

Which service is best suited?
explanation: Application Insights (Azure Monitor) ofrece métricas de uso y Retention que permiten analizar usuarios que regresan a la aplicación. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/usage-retention

question: To what should an application connect to retrieve security tokens?
explanation: Azure AD es el proveedor de identidad que emite tokens (OAuth 2.0/OpenID Connect) para aplicaciones y APIs. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/

question:
You have an Azure environment. You need to create a new Azure virtual machine from a tablet that runs the Android operating system.

Solution: You use PowerShell in Azure Cloud Shell.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Azure Cloud Shell es una experiencia de shell basada en navegador y funciona en dispositivos móviles, incluido Android. Permite ejecutar PowerShell para administrar y crear máquinas virtuales. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-shell/overview

question:
Your company has a Software Assurance agreement that includes Microsoft SQL Server licenses.

You plan to deploy SQL Server on Azure virtual machines.

What should you do to minimize licensing costs for the deployment?
options: Deallocate the virtual machines during off hours.
explanation: Azure Hybrid Benefit permite usar licencias on-premises con Software Assurance habilitado (Windows Server/SQL Server) para reducir costos en Azure VMs/SQL. Referencia: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/hybrid-benefit/

question:
Your company needs to collect and automatically analyze security events from Azure Active Directory (Azure AD).

What should you use?
explanation: Microsoft (Azure) Sentinel es el servicio SIEM/SOAR que ingiere y analiza eventos de seguridad, incluido Azure AD. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/overview

question:
You have an on-premises application that sends email notifications automatically based on a rule. You plan to migrate the application to Azure. You need to recommend a serverless computing solution for the application.

What should you include in the recommendation?
options: a server image in Azure Marketplace
explanation: Azure Logic Apps es una solución serverless para crear flujos de trabajo e integraciones con conectores, como enviar correos cuando se cumplan reglas/trigger. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/logic-apps/logic-apps-overview

question: Which statement accurately describes the Modern Lifecycle Policy for Azure services?
options: Microsoft provides mainstream support for a service for five years.
Microsoft provides a minimum of 12 months' notice before ending support for a service.
After a service is made generally available, Microsoft provides support for the service for a minimum of four years.
When a service is retired, you can purchase extended support for the service for up to five years.
answer: Microsoft provides a minimum of 12 months' notice before ending support for a service.
explanation: La Modern Lifecycle Policy garantiza al menos 12 meses de aviso antes de finalizar soporte si no existe producto sucesor (excluye servicios gratuitos o en vista previa). Referencia: https://support.microsoft.com/help/30881/modern-lifecycle-policy

question:
A team has an Azure CosmosDB account. You need to generate an alert from Azure Log Analytics when a query request charge exceeds 40 units more than 10 times during a 10-minute window.

Which of the following would you recommend? (Choose two.)
options: Create a search query to identify when the requestCharge_s exceeds 40.
Create a search query to identify when the requestCharge_s exceeds 10.
Create a search query to identify when the duration_s exceeds 10.
Configure a period of 10 and a frequency of 10.
answer: Create a search query to identify when the requestCharge_s exceeds 40.
explanation: La respuesta correcta del examen es A y D (se requiere consulta que detecte requestCharge_s > 40 y configurar periodo=10, frecuencia=10). La app valida selección única, por lo que se marca A como correcta y se documenta que también debe configurarse D. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/logging

question:
Your company plans to deploy several web servers and several database servers to Azure. You need to recommend an Azure solution to limit the types of connections from the web servers to the database servers.

What should you include in the recommendation?
explanation: Los NSGs actúan como un firewall para recursos de una red virtual, permitiendo o denegando tráfico entre subredes/VMs mediante reglas de seguridad. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview

question:
You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 and a resource group named RG1. You need to create VM1 in Subscription1 by using the command:

az vm create --resource-group RG1 --name VM1 --image UbuntuLTS --generate-ssh-keys.

From the Azure portal, launch Azure Cloud Shell and select Bash. Run the command.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Azure Cloud Shell proporciona un entorno preconfigurado para ejecutar Azure CLI directamente desde el portal, por lo que se puede crear la VM con ese comando.
