Referencias: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-shell/overview , https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/linux/quick-create-cli

question:
Your company plans to migrate all its network resources to Azure. You need to start the planning process by exploring Azure.

What should you create first?
explanation: Una suscripción de Azure es el contenedor fundamental de recursos y facturación; debe existir antes de crear grupos de recursos o redes. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/ready/azure-best-practices/define-subscriptions

question:
Your company plans to deploy an Artificial Intelligence (AI) solution in Azure.

What should the company use to build, test, and deploy predictive analytics solutions?
explanation: Azure Machine Learning Designer permite crear flujos de trabajo de ML de forma visual para construir, probar y desplegar modelos predictivos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/machine-learning/concept-designer

question:
You have 50 virtual machines hosted on-premises and 50 virtual machines hosted in Azure. The on-premises virtual machines and the Azure virtual machines connect to each other.

Which type of cloud model is this?
explanation: Un modelo de nube híbrida combina recursos locales y de nube pública conectados entre sí. Referencia: https://azure.microsoft.com/overview/what-is-hybrid-cloud-computing/

question:
What are two characteristics of the public cloud? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.

NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
explanation: En la nube pública tienes precios de pago por uso (metered pricing) y autogestión (self-service management). La aplicación admite selección única; además de 'metered pricing', 'self-service management' también es correcto. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/learn/modules/principles-cloud-computing/4-cloud-deployment-models

question:
Your company has an Azure subscription that contains the following unused resources: 20 user accounts in Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), five groups in Azure AD, 10 public IP addresses, and 10 network interfaces.

You need to reduce the Azure costs for the company.

Solution: You remove the unused groups.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Los grupos de Azure Active Directory no generan costos; eliminarlos no reduce el gasto. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/advisor/advisor-cost-recommendations#reduce-costs-by-deleting-or-reconfiguring-idle-virtual-network-gateways

question:
Your company plans to migrate all its data and resources to Azure. The company's migration plan states that only Platform as a Service (PaaS) solutions must be used in Azure. You need to deploy an Azure environment that meets the company migration plan.

Solution: You create an Azure App Service and Azure Storage accounts.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Azure App Service es PaaS, pero las cuentas de Azure Storage se consideran IaaS en este contexto del examen, así que la solución no cumple el plan.

question:
A company has the following on-premise data stores: a Microsoft SQL Server 2012 database and a Microsoft SQL Server 2008 database. The data needs to be migrated to Azure.

Requirement 1: Migrate the SQL Server 2012 database to Azure SQL Database.

Requirement 2: Migrate a table from the SQL Server 2008 database to an Azure CosmosDB account that uses the SQL API.

Which tool should be used to accomplish Requirement 2?
explanation: El Data Migration Tool de Cosmos DB importa datos a cuentas Cosmos DB (SQL API) desde orígenes como SQL Server. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/import-data

question:
Your company plans to migrate all its data and resources to Azure. The company's migration plan states that only Platform as a Service (PaaS) solutions must be used in Azure.

You need to deploy an Azure environment that meets the company migration plan.

Solution: You create an Azure App Service and Azure virtual machines that have Microsoft SQL Server installed.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Las máquinas virtuales son IaaS; instalar SQL Server en VMs no cumple un plan que exige exclusivamente PaaS.

question:
You have an Azure environment that contains multiple Azure virtual machines. You plan to implement a solution that enables the client computers on your on-premises network to communicate to the Azure virtual machines.

Which two Azure resources should you include in the recommendation?

Each correct answer presents part of the solution.

NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
explanation: Para habilitar la conectividad desde la red local hacia las VMs en Azure, se requiere un Virtual Network Gateway desplegado en la subred dedicada 'GatewaySubnet'. Esta pregunta es de selección múltiple (correcto: A y E), pero la aplicación valida una sola opción; por ello se marca el VNG (A) como respuesta y se documenta que también se necesita la subnet de gateway (E). Referencia: https://docs.microsoft.com/office365/enterprise/connect-an-on-premises-network-to-a-microsoft-azure-virtual-network

Solution: You modify an Azure Traffic Manager profile.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Azure Traffic Manager es un balanceador basado en DNS; no expone ni permite tráfico HTTP por sí mismo. Para publicar HTTP (puerto 80) hacia VM1 debe abrirse una regla en el Network Security Group o en Azure Firewall. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/traffic-manager/traffic-manager-overview

question: Which Azure service should you use to collect events from multiple resources into a centralized repository?
explanation: Azure Monitor recopila métricas y registros de múltiples recursos y los centraliza para análisis, alertas y visualización. Event Hubs y Stream Analytics son para ingestión/streaming y procesamiento, y Analysis Services es para modelado semántico BI. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/overview

question:
Your company plans to deploy several custom applications to Azure. The applications will provide invoicing services to the customers of the company. Each application will have several prerequisite applications and services installed.

You need to recommend a cloud deployment solution for all the applications.

What should you recommend?
explanation: IaaS permite aprovisionar infraestructura (VMs, redes, almacenamiento) sobre la cual instalar y configurar requisitos y aplicaciones personalizadas. SaaS entrega aplicaciones listas para usar; PaaS abstrae mucho del entorno y no está pensado para múltiples prerrequisitos específicos. Referencias: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/guide/technology-choices/saas-vs-paas-vs-iaas | https://azure.microsoft.com/overview/what-is-iaas/

question:
You have a resource group named RG1. You plan to create virtual networks and app services in RG1.

You need to prevent the creation of virtual machines only in RG1.

What should you use?
explanation: Azure Policy permite definir reglas que niegan la creación de tipos de recursos específicos (por ejemplo, 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines') en un recurso o grupo de recursos. Un 'lock' bloquearía todo, y los roles/tags no imponen restricciones por tipo de recurso. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/policy/overview

question:
Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. Each question in the series contains a unique solution that might meet the stated goals. Some question-sets might have more than one correct solution, while others might not have a correct solution.

You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You sign in to the Azure portal and create a resource group named RG1. From Azure documentation, you have the following command that creates a virtual machine named VM1: az vm create --resource-group RG1 --name VM1 --image UbuntuLTS --generate-ssh-keys. You need to create VM1 in Subscription1 by using the command.

Solution: From a computer that runs Windows 10, install Azure CLI.

From a command prompt, sign in to Azure and then run the command. Does this meet the goal?
explanation: El comando puede ejecutarse desde PowerShell o símbolo del sistema si se tiene Azure CLI. Sin embargo, debe ejecutarse en el equipo Windows 10, no en Azure Cloud Shell para que coincida con el escenario de la pregunta. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/cli/azure/install-azure-cli-windows

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct. You have several virtual machines in an Azure subscription. You create a new subscription.

/underline The virtual machines cannot be moved to the new subscription. /underline/

Instructions: Review the underlined text. If it makes the statement correct, select 'No change is needed'. If the statement is incorrect, select the answer choice that makes the statement correct.
options: The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription
The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription only if they are all in the same resource group
The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription only if they run Windows Server 2016
answer: The virtual machines can be moved to the new subscription
explanation: Las máquinas virtuales se pueden mover entre suscripciones (y grupos de recursos) dentro de la misma región, cumpliendo requisitos de movimiento de recursos. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/move-resource-group-and-subscription

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

Azure Key Vault is used to store secrets for /underline Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) user accounts. /underline/

Instructions: Review the underlined text. If it makes the statement correct, select 'No change is needed'. If the statement is incorrect, select the answer choice that makes the statement correct.
options: Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) administrative accounts
explanation: Key Vault centraliza secretos de aplicaciones (por ejemplo, cadenas de conexión, certificados) para controlar su distribución y reducir filtraciones accidentales. No es para credenciales de usuarios de Azure AD ni PII. Referencias: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/key-vault/key-vault-overview | https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/manage-secrets-with-azure-key-vault/

question:
Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. Each question in the series contains a unique solution that might meet the stated goals. Some question-sets might have more than one correct solution, while others might not have a correct solution.

After you answer a question in this section, you will NOT be able to return to it. As a result, these questions will not appear in the review screen.

Your Azure environment contains multiple Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP.

Solution: You modify a DDoS protection plan.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: DDoS Protection ayuda a proteger recursos ante ataques; no habilita conectividad HTTP hacia una VM. Para exponer HTTP se debe abrir una regla de NSG o usar Azure Firewall. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/ddos-protection-overview

question:
Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. Each question in the series contains a unique solution that might meet the stated goals. Some question-sets might have more than one correct solution, while others might not have a correct solution.

After you answer a question in this section, you will NOT be able to return to it. As a result, these questions will not appear in the review screen.

You have an Azure environment. You need to create a new Azure virtual machine from a tablet that runs the Android operating system.

Solution: You use the PowerApps portal.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Power Apps permite crear aplicaciones empresariales low/no-code y portales; no se usa para crear máquinas virtuales de Azure. Para ello se puede usar Azure Portal o Azure Cloud Shell. Referencia: https://powerapps.microsoft.com/

question: Which Azure service should you use to store certificates?
explanation: Azure Key Vault es un almacén seguro para secretos sensibles (contraseñas, certificados, claves, tokens y más) respaldado por HSMs validados FIPS 140-2. Requiere autenticación y autorización adecuadas. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/key-vault/key-vault-overview

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

If a resource group named RG1 has a delete lock, /underline only a member of the global administrators group can delete RG1 /underline/.

Instructions: Review the underlined text. If it makes the statement correct, select 'No change is needed'. If the statement is incorrect, select the answer choice that makes the statement correct.
options: the delete lock must be removed before an administrator
an Azure policy must be modified before an administrator
an Azure tag must be added before an administrator
answer: the delete lock must be removed before an administrator
explanation: Los bloqueos (locks) impiden operaciones según su tipo (ReadOnly/Delete). Para eliminar RG1, primero se debe quitar el delete lock. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/lock-resources

question:
Note: This question is part of a series of questions that present the same scenario. Each question in the series contains a unique solution that might meet the stated goals. Some question-sets might have more than one correct solution, while others might not have a correct solution.

After you answer a question in this section, you will NOT be able to return to it. As a result, these questions will not appear in the review screen.

You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that the services running on the virtual machines are available if a single data center fails.

Solution: You deploy the virtual machines to two or more scale sets.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Los scale sets brindan escalado y alta disponibilidad dentro de un dominio de actualización/fallo pero no garantizan resiliencia ante la caída de un datacenter salvo que se configuren explícitamente en múltiples zonas/regiones. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machine-scale-sets/availability

question: What can Azure Information Protection encrypt?
explanation: Azure Information Protection (AIP) clasifica y protege documentos y correos electrónicos mediante etiquetas que aplican cifrado y políticas. Referencias: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/information-protection/what-is-information-protection | https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/information-protection/quickstart-label-dnf-protectedemail

question:
You plan to map a network drive from several computers that run Windows 10 to Azure Storage. You need to create a storage solution in Azure for the planned mapped drive.

What should you create?
options: a Files service in a storage account
a Blobs service in a storage account
answer: a Files service in a storage account
explanation: Azure Files ofrece compartidos SMB accesibles desde Windows y Windows Server mediante unidad de red/UNC. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/files/storage-how-to-use-files-windows

question:
Your company plans to migrate all on-premises data to Azure.

You need to identify whether Azure complies with the company's regional requirements.

What should you use?
explanation: Azure publica certificaciones y cumplimiento regional en el Trust Center/Service Trust Portal. Referencias: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/security/fundamentals/trust-center | https://servicetrust.microsoft.com/

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

Data that is stored in the Archive access tier of an Azure Storage account /underline can be accessed at any time by using azcopy.exe. /underline/
options: can only be read by using Azure Backup
must be restored before the data can be accessed
must be rehydrated before the data can be accessed
answer: must be rehydrated before the data can be accessed
explanation: En la capa Archive los blobs están fuera de línea; deben rehidratarse a Hot/Cool antes de poder leerse. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/blobs/access-tiers-overview

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

Your company implements /underline Azure policies /underline/ to automatically add a watermark to Microsoft Word documents that contain credit card information.
options: Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) Identity Protection
explanation: Azure Information Protection aplica etiquetas que clasifican y protegen documentos, pudiendo añadir marcas visuales como watermarks. Referencia: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/information-protection/quickstart-label-protect

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

You can use /underline Advisor recommendations /underline/ in Azure to send email alerts when the cost of the current billing period for an Azure subscription exceeds a specified limit.
explanation: Budget alerts in Cost Management notify by email when spending exceeds defined thresholds. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/costs/cost-mgt-alerts-monitor-usage-spending

question:
You plan to store 20 TB of data in Azure. The data will be accessed infrequently and visualized by using Microsoft Power BI.

You need to recommend a storage solution for the data.

Which two solutions should you recommend?

Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
explanation: Multi‑response question: correct answers are Azure Data Lake and Azure SQL Data Warehouse (Synapse). Both integrate with Power BI and support large volumes. References: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-lake-store/data-lake-store-power-bi | https://learn.microsoft.com/power-bi/connect-data/service-azure-sql-data-warehouse

question:
Your company plans to migrate to Azure. The company has several departments. All the Azure resources used by each department will be managed by a department administrator.

What are two possible techniques to segment Azure for the departments?

Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
options: multiple Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) directories
explanation: To segment by department you can create separate subscriptions and/or use resource groups to isolate and delegate administration. Regions do not segment responsibilities and multiple AAD directories do not manage resources. References: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/manage/create-subscription | https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/overview

question: What should you use to evaluate whether your company's Azure environment meets regulatory requirements?
explanation: Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly Security Center) assesses compliance and provides a secure score and security recommendations for your resources. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/defender-for-cloud/defender-for-cloud-introduction

question:
You plan to migrate a web application to Azure. The web application is accessed by external users. You need to recommend a cloud deployment solution to minimize the amount of administrative effort used to manage the web application.

What should you include in the recommendation?
explanation: Use Azure App Service (PaaS) to host web apps with minimal operational effort: the platform manages the OS, patching, and scaling. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/overview

question:
You have an Azure environment that contains 10 virtual networks and 100 virtual machines.

You need to limit the amount of inbound traffic to all the Azure virtual networks.

What should you create?
explanation: Azure Firewall lets you centralize and apply access rules across multiple VNets and subscriptions with high availability. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/firewall/overview

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

An Azure region /underline contains one or more data centers that are connected by using a low-latency network. /underline/
options: Is found in each country where Microsoft has a subsidiary office
Can be found in every country in Europe and the Americas only
Contains one or more data centers that are connected by using a high-latency network
explanation: An Azure region contains one or more datacenters connected by a low‑latency regional network within a defined perimeter. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/global-infrastructure/regions/

question: In which type of cloud model are all the hardware resources owned by a third-party and shared between multiple tenants?
explanation: In the public cloud model (Azure, AWS, Google Cloud) the provider owns the infrastructure and multiple tenants share resources that are logically isolated.

question:
Your company plans to move several servers to Azure. The company's compliance policy states that a server named FinServer must be on a separate network segment.

Which Azure solution should you recommend?
options: a resource group for FinServer and another resource group for all the other servers
a virtual network for FinServer and another virtual network for all the other servers
a VPN for FinServer and a virtual network gateway for each other server
one resource group for all the servers and a resource lock for FinServer
answer: a virtual network for FinServer and another virtual network for all the other servers
explanation: Virtual networks (or subnets) are used to segment networks. Placing FinServer in a different VNet than the others guarantees network isolation. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-vnet-plan-design-arm

question:
You need to configure an Azure solution that meets the following requirements: Secures websites from attacks; Generates reports that contain details of attempted attacks.

What should you include in the solution?
explanation: Azure DDoS Protection Standard protects public apps against volumetric attacks and generates detailed reports. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ddos-protection/ddos-protection-overview

question:
You have 1,000 virtual machines hosted on the Hyper-V hosts in a data center. You plan to migrate all the virtual machines to an Azure pay-as-you-go subscription.

You need to identify which expenditure model to use for the planned Azure solution.

Which expenditure model should you identify?
explanation: Migrating to Azure Pay‑As‑You‑Go shifts from CAPEX (own hardware) to OPEX (pay‑as‑you‑go services). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/cloud-adoption/appendix/azure-scaffold

question:
Your company plans to automate the deployment of servers to Azure. Your manager is concerned that you may expose administrative credentials during the deployment.

You need to recommend an Azure solution that encrypts the administrative credentials during the deployment.

What should you include in the recommendation?
explanation: Azure Key Vault securely stores credentials and secrets, enabling controlled access from scripts and templates without exposing values in plain text. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/key-vault/general/overview

question:
Your company plans to purchase Azure. The support policy requires that the Azure environment must provide an option to access support engineers by phone or email.

You recommend a Standard support plan. Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Standard, Professional Direct, and Premier support plans include technical support via email and phone. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/support/plans/

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

You can create an Azure support request from /underline support.microsoft.com. /underline/
explanation: Support requests are created from the Help + Support blade in the Azure portal or from an Azure resource's Support + Troubleshooting menu. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-supportability/how-to-create-azure-support-request

question:
You need to identify the type of failure for which an Azure availability zone can be used to protect access to Azure services.

What should you identify?
explanation: Availability Zones are physically separate datacenters within an Azure region, providing resilience against datacenter-level failures. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/availability-zones/az-overview

question:
You attempt to create several managed Microsoft SQL Server instances in an Azure environment and receive a message that you must increase your Azure subscription limits.

What should you do to increase the limits?
explanation: Quota increases are requested via an Azure support ticket selecting the service and quota type to raise (e.g., SQL Database Managed Instance). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/request-quota-increase

question:
Your company plans to deploy several million sensors that will upload data to Azure. You need to identify which Azure resources must be created to support the planned solution.

Which two Azure resources should you identify? (Choose the correct combination)
options: Azure Data Lake & Azure IoT Hub
Azure Queue Storage & Azure IoT Hub
Azure File Storage & Azure Notification Hubs
Azure Data Lake & Azure Queue Storage
answer: Azure Data Lake & Azure IoT Hub
explanation: Azure IoT Hub ingests device-to-cloud telemetry securely at scale; Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 provides hierarchical namespace and analytics-friendly storage for massive sensor datasets. References: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/iot-hub/iot-hub-devguide-messages-d2c and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/blobs/data-lake-storage-introduction

question:
You have unused resources in Azure: 20 Azure AD user accounts, five groups, 10 public IP addresses, and 10 network interfaces.

You must reduce the company's Azure costs. Solution: Remove the unused public IP addresses.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Public IP addresses are billable; deleting unused public IPs reduces costs. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/ip-addresses/

question:
You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines.

You need to control the ports that devices on the Internet can use to access the virtual machines.

What should you use?
options: an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) role
explanation: Network Security Groups act like a firewall with allow/deny rules for inbound/outbound traffic at NIC or subnet level. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-group-overview

question:
Your company has 10 offices.

You plan to generate several billing reports in the Azure portal, each showing resource utilization for each office.

Which Azure Resource Manager feature should you use before generating the reports?
explanation: Tags are key/value metadata to label resources (e.g., Office=1) and enable filtering and cost reporting by tag. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/tag-resources

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

/underline From Azure Cloud Shell /underline/, you can track your company's regulatory standards and regulations, such as ISO 27001.
explanation: Microsoft Compliance Manager helps assess, track, and verify regulatory compliance for Microsoft cloud services. Azure Cloud Shell is a browser-accessible shell, not a compliance tool. References: https://learn.microsoft.com/microsoft-365/compliance/compliance-manager-overview and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-shell/overview

question:
Your company plans to purchase Azure. The support policy requires options to access support engineers by phone or email.

You recommend a Professional Direct support plan.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Professional Direct, Standard, and Premier support plans include technical support via email and phone. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/support/plans/

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

Azure Germany can be used by /underline legal residents of Germany only. /underline/
options: only enterprises that are registered in Germany
only enterprises that purchase their azure licenses from a partner based in Germany
any user or enterprise that requires its data to reside in Germany
answer: any user or enterprise that requires its data to reside in Germany
explanation: Azure Germany is available to eligible customers and partners globally that require their customer data to be stored exclusively in German datacenters with a data trustee. References: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/germany/germany-welcome and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/germany/germany-overview-data-trustee

question:
Your Azure environment contains multiple Azure virtual machines.

You need to ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP.

Solution: You modify a network security group (NSG).

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Add an inbound NSG rule allowing TCP port 80 (HTTP) to the VM's NIC/subnet. NSGs provide allow/deny rules for inbound/outbound traffic. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/network-security-group-overview

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

When you need to delegate permissions to several Azure virtual machines simultaneously, you must deploy the Azure virtual machines /underline to the same Azure region. /underline/
options: by using the same Azure Resource Manager template
explanation: To delegate permissions across multiple VMs at once, assign RBAC at the resource group scope; region placement is unrelated. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/role-based-access-control/overview

question:
Your Azure environment contains multiple Azure virtual machines.

You need to ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP.

Solution: You modify an Azure firewall.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Configure Azure Firewall DNAT/network rules to allow inbound HTTP (TCP 80) to the VM. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/firewall/overview

question:
You have an Azure environment.

You need to create a new Azure virtual machine from a tablet that runs the Android operating system.

Solution: You use Bash in Azure Cloud Shell.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Azure Cloud Shell is a browser-accessible shell (Bash or PowerShell) that runs in the Azure portal and works on mobile devices, enabling VM creation via CLI. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-shell/overview

question:
Your company plans to purchase Azure. The company's support policy requires an option to access support engineers by phone or email.

Solution: Recommend a Premier support plan.

Does this meet the goal?
explanation: Standard, Professional Direct, and Premier support plans include technical support via email and phone. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/support/plans/

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

/underline Azure Cosmos DB is an example of a platform as a service (PaaS) /underline/ offering.
explanation: Azure Cosmos DB is a fully managed NoSQL database service delivered as PaaS. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/database-security

question: Which Azure service provides a set of version control tools to manage code?
explanation: Azure Repos provides Git and TFVC repositories for code version control within Azure DevOps. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devops/repos/get-started/what-is-repos

question:
An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources.

Which three computers can run the script?

(Choose the correct combination)
explanation: Valid environments: macOS with PowerShell Core, Windows 10 with Azure PowerShell module, and any device using Azure Cloud Shell (supports PowerShell). Linux with only Azure CLI cannot run a PowerShell script. References: https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/install/installing-powershell and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cloud-shell/overview

question:
Your company has an Azure subscription that contains resources in several regions.

You need to ensure that administrators can only create resources in those regions.

What should you use?
explanation: Use Azure Policy to enforce allowed locations so resource creation is restricted to specified regions. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/policy/overview

question:
A company has set up an Azure subscription and an Azure tenant and purchased Premium P2 licenses. Different departments have different identity requirements. For Logistics: Ensure applications hosted on virtual machines can safely access the Azure Key Vault service.

Which of the following would you suggest for the Logistics department?
explanation: Managed Identities for Azure resources allow services/apps on VMs to obtain Azure AD tokens without storing credentials, enabling secure access to Azure Key Vault. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview

question:
This question requires that you evaluate the underlined text to determine if it is correct.

One of the benefits of Azure SQL Data Warehouse is that /underline high availability /underline/ is built into the platform.
explanation: Azure SQL Data Warehouse (Azure Synapse Analytics dedicated SQL pool) is a PaaS service offering a 99.9% availability SLA with built-in high availability. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/synapse-analytics/sql-data-warehouse-overview-faq

question: In which Azure support plans can you open a new support request?
options: Premier, Professional Direct, and Standard only
Premier, Professional Direct, Standard, and Developer only
Premier, Professional Direct, Standard, Developer, and Basic
answer: Premier, Professional Direct, Standard, and Developer only
explanation: Support cases can be opened on Premier, Professional Direct, Standard, and Developer plans. The Basic plan does not include technical support cases. Reference: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/plans/

question:
You need to be notified when Microsoft plans to perform maintenance that can affect the resources deployed to an Azure subscription.

What should you use?
explanation: Azure Service Health provides personalized, authenticated notifications for service issues, planned maintenance, and health advisories that impact your resources. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/service-health/overview

question:
A company has deployed several applications across Windows and Linux virtual machines in Azure. Log Analytics is sending the required data for alerting purposes.

You need to recommend which tables should be queried for security-related queries.

Which of the following would you query for events from Windows Event Logs?
explanation: Windows Event Logs are ingested into the Log Analytics table named 'Event'. Linux syslog data goes to 'Syslog'; Azure Activity captures control-plane operations. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/data-sources-windows-events
